semantic.role: Document defining layered architecture constraints for the ArchSpine project.
responsibilities: Enforce clear separation between CLI entrypoints, service orchestration, core pipeline contracts, task stages, engines, and infrastructure layers.
Provide rules that prevent logic leakage across layers and maintain stable, reusable interfaces.
Guide contributors on where specific types of logic should reside (CLI, services, core, tasks, engines, infra).
outOfScope: Specific implementation details of any module or function.
Business logic or application-specific workflow rules.

English.purpose: To establish and communicate the strict layered architecture rules that keep the ArchSpine codebase maintainable, testable, and extensible. By defining these constraints, the document helps ensure that each architectural layer (CLI, services, core, tasks, engines, infra) only has responsibilities appropriate to its role, preventing tangled dependencies and making the system easier to evolve.
English.context_and_audience: This document is intended for developers and maintainers contributing to the ArchSpine mirror system. It assumes familiarity with the project's modular structure and is used as a reference during code reviews and new feature development to verify that layer boundaries are respected.
key_takeaways: CLI modules must remain thin entrypoints and must not contain pipeline or persistence logic.
Services orchestrate core, tasks, engines, and infra, but view-specific services must live in src/services/view/.
Core modules define stable pipeline contracts and must not depend on CLI code.
Task modules implement stage-local work on top of core contracts and engines, and must not absorb CLI parsing or unrelated service logic.
Engines provide reusable analysis/transformation logic and must stay independent of CLI and service orchestration.
Infra should expose stable, low-level facades, and callers should prefer public facades over deep internal paths.
